core_mechanism: finite-size (demographic) intrinsic noise driving a stochastic dynamical system into long-lived metastable states that differ qualitatively from its deterministic mean-field fixed point, made analytically tractable by timescale separation (adiabatic elimination of the fast variable)
brief_reason: The abstract attributes the emergence of long-lasting polymorphic states to demographic noise in finite populations acting against the deterministic prediction, resolved through a timescale-separation analysis, which is a generic intrinsic-noise-induced metastability mechanism handled by fast-slow adiabatic reduction.
